column1: Draft Strategy Definitions — Detection Rules, Round Windows & Priority Order

Every strategy with its 15R baseline window, detection rule, description, and priority in the overlap resolution chain. Strategies are checked in priority order — first match wins. BPA is always last.

column5: Description
column6: Priority & Overlap Notes

column5: Deliberate avoidance. 0 RBs in first 4 rounds (15R baseline). Relies on WR/TE early, waiver RBs late.
column6: 2nd — broad absence signal. Beats BPA, loses to Hero/Robust/Skill.

column4: Exactly 1 RB in R1, top-6 ADP
column5: One elite bell-cow anchor in round 1, then pivot to WR. Requires top-6 RB ADP to qualify.
column6: 1st — most specific. R1 pick + ADP qualifier makes it unambiguous.

column5: Volume approach. Stack RBs in rounds 1-3. No single player needs to be elite.
column6: 2nd — clustering signal. Checked before Zero RB.

column5: QB or TE in rounds 1-2. Picks 1-2 are always absolute regardless of total rounds.
column6: 3rd — absence of BOTH skill positions. Very specific signal.

column5: Pure value, no positional bias. Default fallback — never first check.
column6: 5th (last) — elimination fallback only.

column4: Exactly 1 WR in R1, top-8 WR ADP
column5: One elite WR anchor in round 1. Requires top-8 WR ADP to qualify.
column6: 1st — most specific. Mutually exclusive with Hero RB.

column5: Aggressively stack WRs in early-middle rounds. Natural complement to Zero RB.
column6: 2nd — clustering in rounds 2-5.

column4: 0 WRs in R1–R2 (absolute); first WR in 13–47% window
column5: Delay WR to middle rounds. Lower bound R1-2 is always absolute.
column6: 3rd — first WR in the middle window.

column5: Punt WR early. Complement to Robust RB. First WR in round 5+.
column6: 4th — broad absence, same structure as Zero RB.

column4: First QB in first 20% of draft
column5: Elite QB anchor. Top-3 QB by ADP. R1-3 in 15R draft.
column6: Checked 1st. Clear early pick signal.

column5: Most common. Balanced approach. Rounds 4-8 in 15R draft.
column6: Checked 2nd. Widest window — default for most drafters.

column5: Intentionally delayed. Rounds 9-12 in 15R draft. Can compress in short drafts.
column6: Checked 3rd. Flag if window < 2 rounds.

column4: First QB in final 20% of draft
column5: Full waiver reliance. Last 3 rounds in 15R. QB taken only when forced.
column6: Checked 4th. Flag if total rounds < 11.

column4: First TE in first 20% of draft
column5: Elite TE premium pick. Rounds 1-3 in 15R. Top-3 TE by ADP.
column6: Checked 1st.

column5: Second-wave TE. Reliable starter. Rounds 4-8 in 15R.
column6: Checked 2nd. Fills the dead middle — most common real behavior.

column5: Full waiver/matchup approach. Round 9+ in 15R.
column6: Checked 3rd.

column5: Boom-or-bust late rounds. Rookies, unclear roles, breakout candidates.
column6: Checked 3rd. Requires player variance tag.

column5: Safe, proven depth. Consistent targets, locked-in roles.
column6: Checked 4th. Requires player floor tag. Default if neither hits 60%.

column4: Picks in 33–80% track top 15% VBD scores
column5: Replacement-level math. Picks consistently outperform their positional baseline.
column6: Checked 2nd. Requires pre-computed VBD scores per player.

column5: Protects RB investments. 2+ picks are tagged backup RBs behind starters.
column6: Checked 1st — most specific. Requires handcuff player-type tag.
